reasoning:
Alireza Firouzja's rating on August 2025 was 2766. A 1% increase requires him to attain a rating of at least 2793.66 (effectively 2794). Gaining 28 FIDE rating points at the super-GM level requires an exceptionally dominant, historic performance. Instead of gaining rating, results from the Sinquefield Cup (held August 18-28, 2025) confirm that Firouzja lost at least two classical games (to Praggnanandhaa and Caruana) and did not win the tournament, meaning his official September FIDE rating will actually decline below 2766.

Because FIDE rankings update strictly on the 1st of each month, his rating on both September 7 and September 30 will certainly be his September rating, which is lower than his August rating. Thus, it is mathematically impossible for him to be +1% up on those dates. 
Looking further ahead to November 2025 and February 2026, Firouzja would need to gain approximately 40+ points to reach 2794, requiring consecutive undefeated tournament victories (e.g., scoring +4 or +5 in events like Tata Steel 2026). While he has reached 2800+ in the past, recovering 40 points in 3-6 months is extremely difficult given current elite competition. I place the probability very low for November and slightly higher for February, acknowledging the longer time frame for potential recovery while staying tethered to base-rate realities of rating mobility at the 2750+ level.
